content:
- Finished the task end-to-end.
- Ran the required Ralph task switch, committed everything as 591cdcd, and pushed to origin/master.
- Post-commit hook rebuilt/published mdBook docs successfully to pgtuskmaster-docs commit f22646bc.
- Working tree is clean after push.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to select the next task under the strict bug-first / blocker rules.
- Found three active failing tasks and zero active failing bugs. Confirmed blockers: cert-reload task is unblocked because ctl task 08 passes true; role-reconciliation task is unblocked because story-config-simplification currently has a single task and it passes true in current_tasks_done.
- Read all three task files. Logging simplification is a broad low-priority refactor; cert reload is a narrow low-priority follow-up on /reload/certs; role reconciliation is a low-priority but newly unblocked dependency continuation from the completed config rewrite.
- Checked current codebase with rg. /reload/certs already exists, logging still has many raw field-bag call sites, and postgres role management is still hard-coded around PostgresRolesConfig { superuser, replicator, rewinder }, ensure_required_roles(...), and required_roles_ready. That makes the role-reconciliation task the most logical next task because it directly continues the finished config work while the code still reflects the old model everywhere.
- Next step was to write .ralph/tasks/story-postgres-role-reconciliation/01-task-make-role-management-config-driven-idempotent-and-reconciling.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately.

- Read previous progress and confirmed the prior task had completed cleanly.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task path for postgres role reconciliation.
- Reading the task file next to determine whether this turn is a TO BE VERIFIED type-design pass or a NOW EXECUTE implementation pass.

- Read the active task file. It has the task description and acceptance criteria, but no explicit TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Need to inspect nearby Ralph task files to infer the expected plan marker/format before editing the task or starting type redesign.

- Confirmed this task is still in pre-execution mode because it lacks a NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Started mapping the current type boundaries in config/schema, postgres_roles, and the HA/runtime call sites so I can do a broad ADT-first redesign before any compile-fix work.

- Mapped the fixed-role seams across config, postgres_roles, HA, and process/runtime types.
- Verifying the explicit task dependency on story-config-simplification before landing the new ADTs, because this task should not start on an unfinished config rewrite.

- Completed the pre-execution ADT redesign pass for the postgres role reconciliation task.
- Changed the config schema to a mandatory-vs-extra role model with PostgresRoleName and ManagedPostgresRoleKey newtypes, plus explicit privilege ADTs for extra managed roles.
- Renamed the HA state/action vocabulary from required_roles_ready / EnsureRequiredRoles to managed_roles_reconciled / ReconcileManagedRoles.
- Reworked the process/runtime role carrier types toward explicit mandatory-role credentials instead of loose strings/profiles.
- Added desired managed-role ADTs and renamed entry points in src/postgres_roles.rs to reconcile_managed_roles / render_managed_role_reconciliation_sql.
- Updated the active task file with the execution plan and appended NOW EXECUTE.
- Did not run compile fixes, checks, lint, tests, docs, task switch, commit, or push because this turn was the type-design-only pass and should stop immediately after landing the plan.
- Next turn should start in NOW EXECUTE mode, fix the intentional compile fallout across call sites/tests/fixtures, then run make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, docs loop, and closeout if the design still holds.

- Started this turn by reading prior progress and the current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is story-postgres-role-reconciliation/01-task-make-role-management-config-driven-idempotent-and-reconciling.md.
- Reading the task file and git status now to confirm whether this is the NOW EXECUTE phase and to see the current worktree before making changes.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE mode.
- The worktree already contains the broad ADT refactor across config, HA, runtime, and postgres role reconciliation files.
- Running git diff --stat and make check now to identify the compile fallout that needs to be fixed before validation.

- make check failed on two String-vs-PostgresRoleName mismatches in pginfo/state.rs and process/source.rs.
- Inspecting the newtype definition and the affected boundaries now so the fix preserves the typed ADT design instead of leaking raw strings back through the model.

- Fixed the first compile breakage by converting PostgresRoleName to String only at PgConnInfo/probe boundaries.
- make check now passes, but it reports unused ADT scaffolding warnings in postgres_roles.rs and an unused accessor in process/state.rs.
- Running make lint next to see whether those warnings must be resolved before moving on to tests.

- make lint fails because the new role ADTs are still dead code: postgres_roles.rs defines the new desired-role types but does not route reconciliation through them yet.
- Reading postgres_roles.rs and the HA/runtime call sites now to decide whether to wire the types through the module or remove redundant scaffolding while keeping the ADT design intact.

- Ran a repo scan for old TOML section headers using the legacy postgres.roles.superuser/replicator/rewinder shape.
- Found stale runtime config examples in docker node configs, the HA world fixture template, and the runtime configuration docs.
- Returning only concrete file and line references to the parent task.

- Reworked src/postgres_roles.rs so DesiredManagedRoleSet now derives from config and drives reconciliation SQL for mandatory plus extra managed roles.
- Added extra-role tagging/drop logic, membership reconciliation, parser-side duplicate username validation, and unit tests for parser and postgres role SQL rendering.
- make check is green again; running make lint now before broader test targets.

- Reworked parser validation to reject duplicate managed-role usernames and added parser tests for reserved keys, duplicates, and empty extra usernames.
- The postgres_roles module now constructs DesiredManagedRoleSet and routes rendering through typed managed-role specs, with marker-based extra-role tracking, membership reconciliation, and stale extra-role drops.
- Running make check now to catch compile fallout from the larger rewrite before moving on to lint and fixture updates.

- make lint code-side is clean under direct clippy, but the orphan-rust-files subtarget is flaky under the temporary target dir; trying single-threaded runs and quieter execution next.
- Checking whether the delegated TOML fixture updates have landed before moving on to test gates, because stale role-shape fixtures will otherwise fail config parsing later.

- make lint now passes after making the orphan-rust-files cargo check deterministic with a fresh serialized target dir in Makefile.
- Updating stale TOML fixtures and samples to postgres.roles.mandatory.* before running make test and make test-long.

- Updated the stale docker and HA runtime-config TOML fixtures to the new postgres.roles.mandatory.* shape.
- make lint is now running successfully under a user systemd unit after fixing two environment issues: the orphan target needed explicit cleanup, and user services needed PATH to include cargo/bin and rg.
- Waiting for the long single-threaded orphan-target/clippy lint pipeline to finish before starting make test and make test-long.

- Resumed the postgres role reconciliation task in NOW EXECUTE mode.
- Re-read the progress log, current task pointer, and task file to confirm only make test-long plus docs/closeout remain.
- Investigating the HA nextest environment mismatch around PGTM_CUCUMBER_TEST_RUN_ID / Docker image tags before any docs or completion steps.

- Narrow HA run reproduced the setup path cleanly with a distinct nextest run id, which points back at shared Docker resource handling rather than the role logic.
- Evidence now strongly suggests the setup script's older-image deletion can race with other nextest invocations and remove the tag an in-flight run still needs.
- Editing tools/nextest/setup-ha-cucumber-image.sh next to stop deleting labeled HA images and to clean up only unused ha-* Docker networks before the long HA run starts.

- All required code/test gates are now green: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long.
- Starting the docs phase and explicitly invoking the k2-docs-loop skill as required by the task instructions.
- Scanning docs for stale postgres role config references while reading the skill instructions.

- Patched tools/nextest/setup-ha-cucumber-image.sh to stop deleting other tagged HA images and to prune only unused ha-* Docker networks before the shared HA image build.
- Cleaned leaked ha-* Docker resources from the prior failed long run and reran make test-long successfully: 32 passed, 7 skipped.
- All validation gates are now green; next step is the required docs update via the k2-docs-loop skill, then Ralph task closeout.

- Completed the postgres role reconciliation task end-to-end, including the post-test docs cleanup and task metadata update.
- Validation passed: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long all succeeded; make lint/test/test-long had to be run through detached systemd units because the terminal wrapper was terminating long jobs.
- Running Ralph task switch now, then I need to commit all tracked/untracked changes for this task and push the branch.
